question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R2 changes its SR Global Block from the default `16000 23999` to
`100000 107999`. R2 now expects to receive labels starting from 100000
for SIDs, but other routers compute labels using their own SRGB
(16000-23999) when pushing toward R2. The label-space mismatch means
R2's SR LFIB cannot resolve incoming labels for transit traffic;
packets to R2-bound SIDs get punted or dropped. Asymmetric SRGB —
classic "operator changed the SR config on one router only" fault.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.102 (R2), the SR Global Block was changed from the default 16000-23999 to 100000-107999.
R2 now allocates and expects SID labels from 100000, but every other router still pushes labels computed from the default SRGB when forwarding toward R2, so the two SRGBs no longer agree on the label-to-SID mapping and R2's SR LFIB cannot resolve incoming transit labels - those packets get punted or dropped.
Fix: remove the custom global-block on R2 (restore the default 16000-23999).
expected_symptoms_summary:
%ROUTING-ISIS-6-SRGB_INFO reported by R2 (2 instances):
- R2: ISIS (LAB): SRGB (index 0, 16000 23999, SRGB_ALLOCATED, 0x1) modified to (100000 107999), global cfg change, disabling SR
- R2: ISIS (LAB): SRGB (index 0, 100000 107999, SRGB_ALLOCATED, 0x0) allocation complete, enabling SR
expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:56:25.218 UTC: isis[1003]: %ROUTING-ISIS-6-SRGB_INFO : ISIS (LAB): SRGB (index 0, 16000 23999, SRGB_ALLOCATED, 0x1) modified to (100000 107999), global cfg change, disabling SR
RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:56:30.254 UTC: isis[1003]: %ROUTING-ISIS-6-SRGB_INFO : ISIS (LAB): SRGB (index 0, 100000 107999, SRGB_ALLOCATED, 0x0) allocation complete, enabling SR
